---
# Generated by scripts/ingest_models.py — do not edit directly.
# Re-run the script to regenerate from ModelAtlasofTheEarth/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents
title: "Strain and retrogression partitioning explain long-term stability of crustal roots in stable continents"
---

```{=html}
<div class="model-page">

  <!-- ── Header ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
  <h1>Strain and retrogression partitioning explain long-term stability of crustal roots in stable continents</h1>

  <div class="model-meta-block">
    <strong>DOI:</strong>
        <a class="badge-doi" href="https://doi.org/10.25914/tm6d-t466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span class="badge-doi-left">DOI</span><span class="badge-doi-right">10.25914/tm6d-t466</span></a>
    <br/><br/>
    <strong>Creators:</strong><br/>
    <a class="badge-creator" href="/creators/bénédicte-cenki-tok-or-cenki.html">Bénédicte Cenki-Tok or Cenki</a>
    <a class="badge-creator" href="/creators/patrice-rey.html">Patrice Rey</a>
    <a class="badge-creator" href="/creators/diane-arcay.html">Diane Arcay</a>
  </div>

  <!-- ── Tabbed content ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
  <div class="tab-container">
    <div class="tab-nav">
      <button class="tab-btn active" onclick="switchTab(this,'snapshot')">Snapshot</button>
      <button class="tab-btn" onclick="switchTab(this,'overview')">Science Overview</button>
      <button class="tab-btn" onclick="switchTab(this,'setup')">Software &amp; Setup</button>
      <button class="tab-btn" onclick="switchTab(this,'data')">Code &amp; Data</button>
      <button class="tab-btn" onclick="switchTab(this,'meta')">Metadata</button>
    </div>

    <!-- Tab 1: Snapshot -->
    <div id="tab-snapshot" class="tab-panel active">
      <p>Away from tectonically active regions, the continental crust has an average thickness of 40 ± 1 km. Yet, it shows a remarkable variability from 25 to 65 km, comparable to that of the most tectonically active regions. Here, we consider the problem of the formation and preservation of anomalous deep crustal roots in stable intracontinental regions. Using two- dimensional thermomechanical experiments, we show that the interplay between partial melting, the formation of garnet-pyroxene-bearing rocks, and their strain rate–dependent retrogression result in the preservation of thick and strong crustal roots. We argue that it is the partitioning into narrow regions of strain, retrogression, and weakening coupled into a positive feedback loop that explains why strong high-grade crustal roots remain largely immune to gravitational stresses and are able to persist over hundreds of millions of years.</p>
      <div class="animation-container">
        <img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ModelAtlasofTheEarth/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents/main/.website_material/graphics/GeolMov.gif"
             alt="Model animation"
             style="max-width:100%; border-radius:6px;"
             onerror="this.style.display='none'" />
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Tab 2: Science Overview -->
    <div id="tab-overview" class="tab-panel">
      <h2>Research Tags</h2>
      <div>
            <a class="badge-tag" href="/tags/crustal-roots.html">crustal roots</a>
            <a class="badge-tag" href="/tags/stability.html">stability</a>
            <a class="badge-tag" href="/tags/retrogression.html">retrogression</a>
      </div>

      <h2>Associated Publication</h2>
        <p>
          <strong>Strain and retrogression partitioning explain long-term stability of crustal roots in stable continents</strong><br/>
          B. Cenki-Tok, P.F. Rey, D. Arcay<br/>
          <em></em><br/>
          <a class="badge-doi" href="https://doi.org/10.1130/g47301.1" onclick="event.stopPropagation()" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span class="badge-doi-left">DOI</span><span class="badge-doi-right">10.1130/g47301.1</span></a>
        </p>

      <h2>Abstract</h2>
      <p>Away from tectonically active regions, the continental crust has an average thickness of 40 ± 1 km. Yet, it shows a remarkable variability from 25 to 65 km, comparable to that of the most tectonically active regions. Here, we consider the problem of the formation and preservation of anomalous deep crustal roots in stable intracontinental regions. Using two-dimensional thermomechanical experiments, we show that the interplay between partial melting, the formation of garnet-pyroxene-bearing rocks, and their strain rate–dependent retrogression result in the preservation of thick and strong crustal roots. We argue that it is the partitioning into narrow regions of strain, retrogression, and weakening coupled into a positive feedback loop that explains why strong high-grade crustal roots remain largely immune to gravitational stresses and are able to persist over hundreds of millions of years.</p>
    </div>

    <!-- Tab 3: Software & Setup -->
    <div id="tab-setup" class="tab-panel">
      <h2>Compute Tags</h2>
      <div>
        <p><em>None specified.</em></p>
      </div>

      <h2>Software</h2>
        <p><strong>Underworld 2</strong></p>

      <h2>Model Setup</h2>
      <div class="full-width-image">
        <img src="https://placehold.co/1200x500/2c8ec7/white?text=Model+Setup"
             alt=""
             style="width:100%; border-radius:6px;"
             onerror="this.src='https://placehold.co/1200x500/2c8ec7/white?text=Model+Setup';" />
      </div>
      
    </div>

    <!-- Tab 4: Code & Data -->
    <div id="tab-data" class="tab-panel">
        <p><strong>Dataset (NCI catalogue):</strong><br/><a href="https://thredds.nci.org.au/thredds/catalog/nm08/MATE/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents/catalog.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener">https://thredds.nci.org.au/thredds/catalog/nm08/MATE/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents/catalog.html</a></p>
        <p><strong>Model files (NCI catalogue):</strong><br/><a href="https://thredds.nci.org.au/thredds/catalog/nm08/MATE/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents/catalog.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener">https://thredds.nci.org.au/thredds/catalog/nm08/MATE/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents/catalog.html</a></p>
        <p><strong>Model files notes:</strong> Code and inputs for computational model</p>
        <p><strong>Source repository:</strong><br/><a href="https://github.com/ModelAtlasofTheEarth/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents" target="_blank" rel="noopener">https://github.com/ModelAtlasofTheEarth/cenki-tok-2020-crustal-roots-in-stable-continents</a></p>
    </div>

    <!-- Tab 5: Metadata -->
    <div id="tab-meta" class="tab-panel">
      <h2>Citation</h2>
      <blockquote>Cenki-Tok or Cenki, B., Rey, P., & Arcay, D. (2024). Strain and retrogression partitioning explain long-term stability of crustal roots in stable continents [Data set]. AuScope, National Computational Infrastructure. https://doi.org/tm6d-t466</blockquote>

      <h2>Licence</h2>
      <p>
        <a href="https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode" target="_blank" rel="noopener">CC-BY-4.0</a>
      </p>

      <h2>Funders</h2>
      <ul>
    <li>European Regions Research and Innovation Network</li>
  </ul>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>

<script>
function switchTab(btn, id) {
  var container = btn.closest('.tab-container');
  container.querySelectorAll('.tab-btn').forEach(function(b) { b.classList.remove('active'); });
  container.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel').forEach(function(p) { p.classList.remove('active'); });
  btn.classList.add('active');
  container.querySelector('#tab-' + id).classList.add('active');
}
</script>
```
